title: QUIC Interop Harness Adapter Contract
purpose: Define the interop-runner-facing companion harness contract that hosts Incursa.Quic without moving protocol/runtime ownership out of the main library.
scope: This slice covers the harness project shape, runner environment parsing, mounted path mapping, unsupported testcase exit behavior, placeholder diagnostics hooks, Docker packaging, and CI participation.
context: Incursa.Quic remains the owner of reusable transport/runtime behavior. The interop harness only exists to satisfy the process, environment, filesystem, and exit-code contract expected by the QUIC interop runner.
open_questions: Which application protocol and ALPN should the eventual file-transfer endpoint use once Incursa.Quic can drive real end-to-end interop behavior?
When a real TLS provider is selected, what is the practical path for optional SSLKEYLOGFILE support without leaking provider-specific details into the harness?

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Support Posture
supplemental_sections.content: No end-to-end interop testcases are currently supported. The harness must return exit code `127` for `handshake`, `transfer`, `retry`, and every other unsupported testcase until Incursa.Quic can execute them for real.

supplemental_sections.heading: Boundary Split
supplemental_sections.content: Library-owned concerns include connection runtime, sender and recovery ownership, TLS-facing transport state, packet protection lifecycle, handshake confirmation, retransmission planning, timer ownership, path-state ownership, and transport-visible diagnostics hooks. Harness-owned concerns include environment parsing, mounted path mapping, role dispatch, process exit codes, Docker packaging, and startup scripts.

requirements.title: Keep the interop endpoint in a companion project
requirements.statement: The interop endpoint MUST be implemented as a companion project that references `Incursa.Quic` rather than by turning the main library project into an application.
notes: The harness exists to host the library in the process shape expected by the interop runner.
Reusable transport behavior remains in the library.

requirements.title: Own runner-facing process plumbing in the harness
requirements.statement: The interop harness MUST own parsing of `ROLE`, `TESTCASE`, `REQUESTS`, `QLOGDIR`, and `SSLKEYLOGFILE`, mapping of `/www`, `/downloads`, and `/certs`, role dispatch, and runner-facing process exit-code behavior.
notes: These concerns exist because of the interop runner contract and would not belong in the library.

requirements.title: Return unsupported testcases honestly
requirements.statement: The interop harness MUST return exit code `127` for any unsupported or not-yet-implemented interop testcase, including `handshake`, `transfer`, and `retry` until `Incursa.Quic` can execute those cases end to end for real.

requirements.title: Keep qlog and keylog hooks honest
requirements.statement: When `QLOGDIR` is provided, the harness MUST wire that setting into a diagnostics placeholder hook without hardwiring the library to qlog JSON, and when `SSLKEYLOGFILE` is not yet practical it MUST remain a clear TODO rather than fake behavior.

requirements.title: Reject invalid runner configuration with failure status
requirements.statement: The interop harness MUST reject invalid `ROLE`, missing `TESTCASE`, or other runner-configuration failures that prevent dispatch with exit code `1` instead of reporting unsupported success.
